Ohùn mi jẹ́ ẹ̀bùn láti ọ̀run wá.
Oore Ọlọ́run kò lópin rárá.
Oore-ọ̀fẹ́ ló gbé mi kọjá ìjàkadì náà.
Àánú Olúwa ni kò jẹ́ kí n ṣubú.
Ojú mi wà ní ọ̀run níbi tí ìrètí mi gbé wà.
Ìgbàgbọ́ mi kò ní wárìrì bó tilẹ̀ jẹ́ pé ọ̀nà kún fún òkùnkùn.
Ìgbàlà mi dé lọ́jọ́ tí mo yíjú padà.
Ìrètí mi wà lára ẹni tí kò lè purọ́.
Ìfẹ́ Ọlọ́run bò mí mọ́lẹ̀ bí aṣọ.
Ògo ni fún Ọba tí kò ní ikú.
Ògo ni fún Ọlọ́run, ó ṣe ohun ńlá.
Ẹ jẹ́ ká gbé ohùn sókè: Halẹlúyà!
Yìn Ọlọ́run pẹ̀lú gbogbo ọkàn rẹ.
Ìyìn Olúwa kún ẹnu mi ní àárọ̀ àti alẹ́.
Ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ọpẹ́ fún àánú tí kò tán.
Ẹ̀mí mi ń fò sókè nínú ìyìn.
Ọkàn mi, yin Olúwa, má gbàgbé oore rẹ̀.
Àlàáfíà Ọlọ́run bo ilé mi mọ́lẹ̀.
Ààbò Olúwa ni odi tí ó yí mi ká.
Ó jẹ́ àpáta mi nígbà ìjì líle.
Olùgbàlà mi kò ní fi mí sílẹ̀.
Olùṣọ́ àgùntàn mi ń darí mi sí ibi tútù.
Ọba tí ń bẹ láéláé, ìtẹ́ rẹ kò ní wó.

meaning: Your Majesty (royal acclamation, extended to God)
example: Kábíyèsí o, Ọba tí kò séni tó lè bi léèrè.
Alágbára tó dá ayé, ọwọ́ rẹ̀ tún wà lórí mi.
Kádàrá mi rere kò ní yẹ̀.
Ààyè tí mo ní lónìí tó láti fi dúpẹ́.
Ìdáǹdè mi dé, ẹ̀wọ̀n ti já.
Ìwòsàn Olúwa dé sára mi ní àṣálẹ́.
Ìjọsìn mi kún fún ọkàn tòótọ́.

meaning: to place a matter before God / commit it
example: Mo fi ọ̀rọ̀ mi lélẹ̀ lọ́wọ́ Ọlọ́run.

meaning: clap your hands for the Lord
example: Ẹ fọwọ́ pàtẹ́wọ́ fún Olúwa tó ṣẹ́gun.
Kí Ọlọ́run mú un dé ọ̀run rere.
Ẹ̀kún àdúrà rẹ̀ dé ọ̀dọ̀ Olúwa.
Àánú Jésù ni mo fi ń gbé ẹ̀mí ró.

meaning: blessing, prosperity (from Arabic, used esp. by Yoruba Muslims)
example: Alubarika Ọlọ́run wà lórí iṣẹ́ ọwọ́ mi.

meaning: it is peace / all is well
example: Bí wọ́n bá bi mí, àlàáfíà ni mo máa fèsì.
Ọlọ́run ṣeun, ìdààmú náà ti kọjá.
Ẹni ìgbàlà kì í dẹ́rù ba ikú.
Àjíǹde ni ìrètí àwa onígbàgbọ́.
Ọwọ́ Ọlọ́run ṣí sílẹ̀ láti gbé mi ró.

term: Bí a ṣe ń ṣe ní ilé wa
meaning: 'As we do it in our own house' — every household/culture has its own way of doing things
example: Bí a ṣe ń ṣe ní ilé wa, mo mú un wá sí orin yìí.

term: Àgbà kì í wà lọ́jà kí orí ọmọ tuntun wọ́
meaning: An elder does not sit idly in the market while a newborn's head hangs crooked — the wise won't let disaster happen in their presence
example: Àgbà kì í wà lọ́jà, torí náà mo dúró fún ọ.

meaning: Words are worth more than money — talk/counsel carries great weight
example: Ọ̀rọ̀ pọ̀ ju owó lọ, gbọ́ tèmi kó tó pẹ́.

term: Ẹni tí ó bá dárí jì, á rí ìdáríjì
meaning: Whoever forgives will himself receive forgiveness
example: Ẹni tí ó bá dárí jì á rí ìdáríjì, torí náà jẹ́ ká ronú.

term: Ilé ọba tó jó, ẹwà ló bù sí i
meaning: When the king's palace burns down, it only adds to its beauty — a setback can turn into greater glory
example: Ilé ọba tó jó, ẹwà ló bù sí i, má sunkún mọ́.

term: Bí ọmọdé bá ṣubú, á wo iwájú; bí àgbà bá ṣubú, á wo ẹ̀yìn
meaning: When a child falls he looks forward; when an elder falls he looks back — youth press on, elders reflect on the cause
example: Bí ọmọdé bá ṣubú á wo iwájú, ṣùgbọ́n èmi wo ẹ̀yìn.

term: Àìsí ẹkùn, ìkookò ń jẹ ọba
meaning: In the absence of the leopard, the hyena reigns — when the strong is away, the weak takes charge
example: Àìsí ẹkùn, ìkookò ń jẹ ọba nínú ìlú náà.

term: Ọwọ́ ọmọdé kò tó pẹpẹ, ti àgbàlagbà kò wọ akèrègbè
meaning: A child's hand cannot reach the high shelf, an elder's hand won't fit in the gourd — everyone has their own limits, we need each other
example: Ọwọ́ ọmọdé kò tó pẹpẹ, torí náà ẹ jẹ́ ká fọwọ́sowọ́pọ̀.

term: Ẹni a fẹ́ là ń mọ̀, a kì í mọ ẹni tó fẹ́ni
meaning: We know whom we love, but not who loves us — love given isn't always love returned
example: Ẹni a fẹ́ là ń mọ̀, mo ti kọ́ ẹ̀kọ́ nínú ìfẹ́.

term: Àgbájọ ọwọ́ ni a fi ń sọ̀yà
meaning: It is the gathering of fingers that beats the chest — unity gives strength
example: Àgbájọ ọwọ́ ni a fi ń sọ̀yà, ẹ jẹ́ ká ṣọ̀kan.

term: Owó kì í wọ àpò ọ̀lẹ
meaning: Money never enters a lazy person's pocket — reward follows work
example: Owó kì í wọ àpò ọ̀lẹ, dìde kí o ṣiṣẹ́.

term: Ọ̀rọ̀ àgbà kì í wó lulẹ̀
meaning: The word of an elder does not fall to the ground unfulfilled
example: Ọ̀rọ̀ àgbà kì í wó lulẹ̀, gbọ́ ohun tí bàbá sọ.

term: Bí a bá fi ọwọ́ òsì tọ́ ọmọ, ọwọ́ ọ̀tún ni a fi ń fà á mọ́ra
meaning: If we push a child away with the left hand, we draw him close with the right — correction and love go together
example: Bí a bá fi ọwọ́ òsì tọ́ ọmọ, a máa fi ọ̀tún fà á mọ́ra.

meaning: Ignorance has no shame — the one who doesn't know isn't embarrassed to ask
example: Àìmọ̀kan ò ní ìtìjú, bèèrè kí o tó ṣe.

term: Ẹni tó jìn sí kòtò, kọ́ ará yòókù lọ́gbọ́n
meaning: He who falls into a pit teaches others wisdom — one person's mistake is a lesson for all
example: Ẹni tó jìn sí kòtò kọ́ ará yòókù, má gbàgbé ẹ̀kọ́ náà.

term: Kòkòrò tó ń jẹ efọ́, ara efọ́ ló wà
meaning: The insect eating the vegetable lives right inside the vegetable — the trouble comes from within/among us
example: Kòkòrò tó ń jẹ efọ́ ara efọ́ ló wà, ọ̀tá wà nínú ilé.

term: Ọ̀gbọ́n ọlọ́gbọ́n kì í jẹ́ kí a pe àgbà ní wèrè
meaning: Another's cleverness should not make us call an elder a fool — respect the wisdom of age
example: Ọ̀gbọ́n ọlọ́gbọ́n kì í jẹ́ ká pe àgbà ní wèrè, bọ̀wọ̀ fún wọn.

term: Bí ọwọ́ bá mọ́ ẹnu, ẹnu a mọ́ ọwọ́
meaning: If the hand is clean to the mouth, the mouth is clean to the hand — mutual honesty and fair dealing
example: Bí ọwọ́ bá mọ́ ẹnu, ẹnu a mọ́ ọwọ́, jẹ́ ká ṣòótọ́.

term: A kì í fi ọ̀rọ̀ pamọ́ sínú, kí a lè yanjú rẹ̀
meaning: You cannot solve a matter by hiding it inside — speak the problem to settle it
example: A kì í fi ọ̀rọ̀ pamọ́ sínú, sọ̀rọ̀ jáde.
Ìfẹ́ ju owó lọ, ọ̀rẹ́ mi.

meaning: The day we die is our real deathlessness — a good name outlives us
example: Ọjọ́ tí a kú làìkú, jẹ́ ká fi orúkọ rere sílẹ̀.

term: Bí a bá ṣe rere, à á rí rere
meaning: If we do good, we will find good
example: Bí a bá ṣe rere à á rí rere, má rẹ̀wẹ̀sì.

term: Ọmọ tí ò ní ìyá, ẹyẹ ló ń tọ́jú rẹ̀
meaning: The motherless child is raised by the birds — heaven cares for the abandoned
example: Ọmọ tí ò ní ìyá, ọ̀run ló ń tọ́jú rẹ̀.

meaning: Character is the first thing in a person — conduct comes before all else
example: Ìwà lẹwà, àkọ́kọ́ ẹ̀dá ni ìwà.

meaning: Character is beauty — good conduct is true beauty
example: Ìwà lẹwà, kì í ṣe awọ̀ ojú lásán.

meaning: Patience is the father of all virtues
example: Sùúrù ni baba ìwà, fi sùúrù ṣe ohun gbogbo.

term: Ẹni tó ní sùúrù ló ń fún wàrà kìnnìún
meaning: Only the patient one can milk a lioness — patience achieves the impossible
example: Ẹni tó ní sùúrù ló ń fún wàrà kìnnìún, má kánjú.

term: Ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ọwọ́ ní ń mú iṣẹ́ fúyẹ́
meaning: Many hands make the work light
example: Ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ọwọ́ ní ń mú iṣẹ́ fúyẹ́, ẹ wá ràn mí lọ́wọ́.

term: A kì í kùnà kí a wá bẹ̀rẹ̀
meaning: One does not fail before even beginning — don't give up before you try
example: A kì í kùnà kí a wá bẹ̀rẹ̀, gbìyànjú kúkú.

term: Ọ̀rọ̀ ọmọdé kì í kún inú àgbà
meaning: A child's words do not fill an elder's belly — the young lack the depth of the old
example: Ọ̀rọ̀ ọmọdé kì í kún inú àgbà, jẹ́ ká gbọ́ ti àgbà.

term: Ẹni tó bá mọ ọ̀nà kì í ṣìnà
meaning: One who knows the road does not lose his way — knowledge prevents error
example: Ẹni tó bá mọ ọ̀nà kì í ṣìnà, tẹ̀lé ẹni tó mọ̀ ọ́n.

meaning: Silence is the beginning of wisdom in speech — think before you talk
example: Àìlóhùn ni ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ ọ̀rọ̀, dákẹ́ kí o tó dáhùn.

term: Bí ẹrú bá ń jẹun, kì í rántí ọjọ́ ọ̀la
meaning: When a slave eats, he forgets tomorrow — the shortsighted enjoy now and ignore the future
example: Má jẹ bí ẹrú tó ń gbàgbé ọjọ́ ọ̀la.

term: Ọ̀tá ẹni kì í fẹ́ kí ẹni dàgbà
meaning: Your enemy never wants you to grow/prosper
example: Ọ̀tá ẹni kì í fẹ́ kí ẹni dàgbà, ṣùgbọ́n mo tẹ̀síwájú.

term: Ẹni tí Ọlọ́run bù kún, kò sí ẹni tó lè gán
meaning: Whom God has blessed, no one can curse
example: Ẹni tí Ọlọ́run bù kún, kò sí ẹni tó lè gán, mo dúpẹ́.

term: Ohun tí ẹni ń wá lọ Sókótó, ó wà lápò ṣòkòtò
meaning: What one travels all the way to Sokoto to seek is right there in the trouser pocket — what you chase far off is often close by
example: Ohun tí o ń wá lọ Sókótó, ó wà lápò ṣòkòtò rẹ.

term: Ẹnu ni ọ̀rọ̀ ti í jáde
meaning: It is from the mouth that trouble/words come out — guard your speech
example: Ẹnu ni ọ̀rọ̀ ti í jáde, ṣọ́ ẹnu rẹ.

term: Bí ojú ò bá rí, ẹnu kì í sọ
meaning: If the eye hasn't seen it, the mouth shouldn't speak it — don't testify to what you didn't witness
example: Bí ojú ò bá rí, ẹnu kì í sọ, má parọ́.

term: Igi ganganran má gún mi lójú, òkèèrè la ti ń wò ó
meaning: A sharp stick that could pierce the eye is watched from afar — deal with danger before it reaches you
example: Igi ganganran, òkèèrè la ti ń wò ó, kìlọ̀ ní àkókò.

term: Àgbàdo tó dàgbà, orí rẹ̀ á tẹ̀ba
meaning: The ripe maize bows its head — greatness brings humility
example: Àgbàdo tó dàgbà orí rẹ̀ á tẹ̀ba, jẹ́ ẹni pẹlẹ.
Ẹ̀kọ́ ò ní ìparí, máa kọ́ ọ̀gbọ́n títí ayé.

term: Ọjọ́ pípẹ́ ní ń san ìṣẹ́ àìsàn
meaning: Time (long days) heals the wound of sickness — patience with time brings recovery
example: Ọjọ́ pípẹ́ ní ń san ìṣẹ́, fi àkókò sílẹ̀.

meaning: Not only one road leads to the market — there is more than one way to succeed
example: Ọ̀nà kán ò wọ ọjà, wá ọ̀nà mìíràn.

term: Ẹni tó bá fẹ́ jẹ oyin inú àpáta, kò gbọdọ̀ wo ẹnu àáké
meaning: Whoever wants honey from the rock must not mind the blunting of the axe — great reward requires sacrifice
example: Ẹni tó fẹ́ jẹ oyin inú àpáta kò gbọdọ̀ wo ẹnu àáké, ṣiṣẹ́ takuntakun.

term: Bí a ò bá kú, ìṣẹ́ ò tán
meaning: As long as we are not dead, there is still hope — while there's life there's a chance
example: Bí a ò bá kú, ìṣẹ́ ò tán, má fi ìrètí sílẹ̀.

term: Àṣírí tó bá tú, kò lè bọ́ mọ́
meaning: A secret once exposed cannot be hidden again
example: Àṣírí tó bá tú kò lè bọ́ mọ́, ṣọ́ra fún ohun tí o ń sọ.

term: Ọwọ́ tí a bá nà sí ọ̀run, ilẹ̀ ni yóò padà bọ̀
meaning: The hand stretched up to heaven must come back down to earth — pride will humble itself
example: Ọwọ́ tí a bá nà sí ọ̀run, ilẹ̀ ni yóò padà bọ̀, má gбéga.

term: Ẹni tó gbin ẹ̀gún, ẹsẹ̀ rẹ̀ ni yóò kàn
meaning: Whoever plants thorns will step on them himself — evil returns to its doer
example: Ẹni tó gbin ẹ̀gún, ẹsẹ̀ rẹ̀ ni yóò kàn, ṣọ́ra fún ìwà búburú.

term: Ọ̀rẹ́ tòótọ́ ni a mọ̀ nígbà ìṣòro
meaning: A true friend is known in time of trouble
example: Ọ̀rẹ́ tòótọ́ ni a mọ̀ nígbà ìṣòro, mo mọ̀ ọ́ lónìí.

term: Ọ̀gẹ̀dẹ̀ tó bá pọ́n, kì í dúró lórí igi
meaning: The ripe banana does not stay on the tree — when the time comes, things must move on
example: Ọ̀gẹ̀dẹ̀ tó bá pọ́n kì í dúró lórí igi, àsìkò dé.

term: Bí a bá jọ jẹ, à á jọ tọ́jú
meaning: If we eat together, we should care for each other together — shared blessings, shared responsibility
example: Bí a bá jọ jẹ, à á jọ tọ́jú, ẹ jẹ́ ká fọwọ́sowọ́pọ̀.

term: Ẹni tó ní kí ọ̀rọ̀ dára, kò ní kí ọ̀rọ̀ pọ̀
meaning: He who wants speech to be good does not want it to be plentiful — few well-chosen words are best
example: Ẹni tó ní kí ọ̀rọ̀ dára kò ní kí ọ̀rọ̀ pọ̀, sọ̀rọ̀ díẹ̀.

term: Àdììtú kì í pẹ́ kí ó tú
meaning: A tangled knot will eventually be untangled — every mystery is revealed in time
example: Àdììtú kì í pẹ́ kí ó tú, òtítọ́ á farahàn.

term: Ẹni a bí lọmọ, kò gbọdọ̀ hùwà ẹrú
meaning: One born a freeborn child must not behave like a slave — live up to your dignity
example: Ẹni a bí lọmọ kò gbọdọ̀ hùwà ẹrú, mọ iyì rẹ.

term: Bí abẹ́ bá mú tó, kò lè gbẹ́ èkù ara rẹ̀
meaning: However sharp a blade is, it cannot carve its own handle — no one is self-sufficient
example: Bí abẹ́ bá mú tó, kò lè gbẹ́ èkù ara rẹ̀, a nílò ara wa.

term: Ọjọ́ gbogbo kì í dọjọ́ ìdùn
meaning: Not every day is a day of joy — good and bad times alternate
example: Ọjọ́ gbogbo kì í dọjọ́ ìdùn, mú sùúrù.

term: Ọmọ ẹní kì í burú títí, kí a fi fún ẹkùn pa jẹ
meaning: However bad one's child is, you don't hand him to a leopard to devour — you protect your own despite faults
example: Ọmọ ẹní kì í burú títí kí a fi fún ẹkùn, mo dáàbò bò ó.

meaning: The beginning of wisdom is the fear of God
example: Ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ ọgbọ́n ni ìbẹ̀rù Ọlọ́run, bẹ̀rẹ̀ níbẹ̀.

term: A kì í mọ orí olóyè lásìkò àdéhùn
meaning: You do not recognise who will become chief at the time of promise — the future is hidden
example: A kì í mọ orí olóyè, jẹ́ ká bọ̀wọ̀ fún gbogbo ènìyàn.

term: Ọ̀rọ̀ tí ó ba ilé jẹ́, inú ilé ni ń bẹ̀rẹ̀
meaning: The trouble that ruins a home starts from within the home
example: Ọ̀rọ̀ tí ó ba ilé jẹ́, inú ilé ni ń bẹ̀rẹ̀, ẹ ṣọ́ra.

term: Ẹni tó dúpẹ́ ọrẹ àná, á rí ọrẹ ọ̀la gbà
meaning: He who gives thanks for yesterday's gift will receive tomorrow's gift too — gratitude attracts more blessing
example: Ẹni tó dúpẹ́ ọrẹ àná á rí ti ọ̀la, dúpẹ́ nígbà gbogbo.
Kedu, my guy, how far the hustle today?
Kedu ka i mere since we last linked up?
Ndeewo, elders, we thank you for coming.
Daalu for holding me down when things were tight.
Imela, you really carried this one on your back.
Jisike, brother, the grind is going to pay off soon.
Ndo, I know that loss hit you deep.
Biko, hold my hand small before the month runs out.

meaning: father; also used like 'bro/man' to a male friend
example: Nna, this life na waya, we go still make am.

meaning: mother; also affectionate 'my dear' to a woman
example: Nne, your smile is the sweetest thing in this city.

meaning: exclamation of shock, pity or awe
example: Chai, see how far we have come from nothing.
Chineke, this thing you did shocked my whole soul.
Chukwu is the one holding my life together.

meaning: my personal god / my guardian spirit
example: Chi m, do not let my enemies laugh at me.
Obi m, come closer, let the night hold us.
Ife m, no distance can cut this rope between us.
That asa walked in and the whole room went quiet.
Her dance carry mma that money cannot buy.
See this oyoyo girl making my heart run fast.
This nwa get sense pass many old heads.
A real nwoke stands by his word till the end.
This nwanyi runs her business like a general.
Nwa baby, calm down, the night is still young.
Oga, the money don land, let us start the show.
Odogwu don enter, clear road for the boss.
They call me ochiagha because I never run from battle.
From gutter to ogaranya, na God do am for me.

meaning: apprentice / servant boy under a trader
example: I was a nwa boi before I built my own empire.
Ego dey come, ego dey go, but respect na forever.
Sit down make I gift you the akuko of my journey.
No bring wahala for my door, I want my peace.
Mba, I will not sell my dream for small change.
Ee, I hear you loud, we move tonight.

meaning: amen / so be it (response to prayer)
example: May your name shine, and everyone answered isee.
Ofensibe I close this deal, we celebrate hard.
Nwanne, hold my back and I hold yours.

meaning: my brother / my sister (term of closeness)
example: Nwanne m, we started this road together, we finish it together.
My padi, na you sabi my real story.
That guy man knows every corner of the city.

meaning: you hear? / do you understand? (tag at sentence end)
example: Do it well, inu, I no want excuse.
That nne mmuo fears nobody in this market.

meaning: mad person / crazy one (can be playful insult)
example: You dey dance like onye ara, but I love the energy.
My ndi ala crew turn every party upside down.
Oshey baba, you carry the whole show today.

meaning: goodbye / see you later (till it happens)
example: Ka omesia, my people, keep the fire burning.
Ka chi fo, rest well, tomorrow is ours.
Nno, my brother, this house is your house.
This nwa mba no dey bow for pressure.
Ozugbo the beat drops, everybody hit the floor.
Nsogbu adighi, we settle everything with love.
Ututu oma, city of hustlers, rise and shine.
Ehihie oma, let us break bread before the meeting.
Mgbede oma, the lights are on, the party starts.
He walks like oke mmadu wherever he lands.
Nwoke oma, your good heart will open big doors.

meaning: first daughter; affectionate name for a woman
example: Ada of the land, your beauty rings like a bell.
Nwa ada, walk tall, this whole town is yours.
Omalicha, the moon dey envy your glow.
You be iheoma wey enter my life without warning.
When the egwu starts, my legs no fit stay still.
My name dey ring gburugburu this whole state.
Ndu is short, so I chase my dream like fire.

M hụrụ gị n'anya karịa ka okwu nwere ike ịkọwa.
Bịa nʼebe a, obi m, ka m makụọ gị.

term: ọ bụ gị ka m chọrọ

Ọmalicha m, ọ dịghị onye yiri gị n'ọhịa a.
Asa m, ihu gị na-eme ka ehihie dịrị mma.

meaning: I miss you / I long for you
example: Kemgbe ị hapụrụ, ọ na-agụ m gị nke ukwuu.

meaning: a longing for you consumes me
example: N'abalị niile, agụụ gị na-agụ m ruo mgbe chi bọrọ.
Mgbe ị lara, obi m tọrọ nʼala.
Okwu ahụ i kwuru gbawara m obi.
Ihu ọma gị na-eme ka m chefuo nsogbu m.
Nwaanyị mara mma dị ka gị bụ onyinye.
Nwoke m, ị bụ ike na ndo m.
Di m, ọ bụ gị ka m họọrọ ruo ebighị ebi.
Nwunye m, gị bụ ihe kachasị m mma.
Amaara m na ọ hụrụ m n'anya site n' anya ya.

meaning: eyes that show love / a loving gaze
example: Anya na-ahụ n'anya gị na-agwa m eziokwu.
Onye m, ọ dịghị ihe ga-ekewa anyị.

meaning: you are in my heart / I hold you dear
example: Ebe ọ bụla m gara, ọ dị m nʼobi.
Enweghị gị, ị bụ ndụ m, m gaghị adị.

meaning: joyful heart / heart full of gladness
example: Ịhụ gị na-enye m obi anụrị mgbe niile.

term: m ga-ahụ gị n'anya ruo ebighị ebi
example: Ekwem gị nkwa: m ga-ahụ gị n'anya ruo ebighị ebi.

meaning: I like him/her / it pleases me
example: Site na mbụ m hụrụ ya, ọ masịrị m.

meaning: I'm crazy about you (lit. I could die for you)
example: Nwa a, m nwụọ n'ihi gị, ọ bụ gị bụ everything.
Nwaanyị a browbrow, ọ na-eme ka obi na-alọ m.

meaning: it delights me / you sweeten me
example: Ịnọ n'akụkụ gị na-atọ m ụtọ karịa ihe ọ bụla.
Mmadụ m, kwụsị iju m ajụjụ, naanị gị ka m maara.
M ji obi ọcha hụ gị n'anya, enweghị aghụghọ.
Ana m enye gị ịhụnanya m niile, ọ dịghị nke fọdụrụ.
Kemgbe abalị, ọ gụsịrị m agụụ ka m fụ gị anya.

meaning: my precious one (lit. my personal god — used affectionately)
example: Chim, gị onwe gị bụ ọṅụ nke obi m.

meaning: the one my heart belongs to
example: Onye obi m, m na-eche gị ka ọchịchịrị na-agba.
Ị bụ ihe ịhụnanya m, mkpuru anya m.

meaning: I cherish you / you matter to me
example: Chọpụta na m ji gị kpọrọ ihe karịa ego.
Ọ bụ ezie na anyị dị anya, obi m na-amakụ gị.

meaning: young lady / girl (often the one being courted)
example: Nwa agbọghọ ahụ bidoro obi m ka mbụ m hụrụ ya.

Mkpụrụ obi m, ọ dịghị ebe m ga-aga na-echeghị gị.
Kwa ụtụtụ, ana m echeta gị tupu m mepee anya.

meaning: I have a crush on / I'm feeling him/her
example: Site mgbe niile, nwa a ọ na-emi m nke ọ bụghị obere.
Nye anyị, ọ bụ ihunanya nʼanya mbụ n'ahịa ahụ.
Ọ bụ naanị gị, obi m na-akụ n'ihi gị.

term: ọ bụ gị bụ nke m

Ọ bụrụgodụ na ihe niile daa, m gaghị ahapụ gị.

meaning: it touched my heart / struck me deeply

meaning: in my eyes / to me (as in 'beautiful in my eyes')
example: N'anya m, ọ dịghị onye mara mma karịa gị.

meaning: you turn me on / set me on fire (desire)
example: Etu ị si ele m anya ọ na-akpọ m ọkụ.

meaning: good/beautiful child (sweet address to a lover)
example: Nwa ọma, jikere ka anyị pụọ n'anyasị a.

meaning: my heart is full of you
example: Ebe ọ bụla m lere anya, obi m juru gị.
Ọ bụghị inwe ego, ọ bụ gị ka ana m acho.

meaning: you are my beauty / my babe
example: Gị bụ mma m, ọ dịghị mgbanwe na nke ahụ.
Mgbe ọ bụla ị batara, obi na-agba m gharịị.

meaning: my treasure / what my eyes hold dear
example: Ị bụ ihe m ji anya, agaghị m eku gị ekekwu.
Karịa ka ị chere, ọ dị m mkpa nke ukwuu.

Ụbịam bụ agha m na-alụ kwa ụbọchị.

meaning: be strong, persevere, keep at it

Gbasie ike n'ọrụ gị, uru ga-abịa.
Ọ bụ ọrụ na-eweta ego, ọ bụghị ekwurekwu.

meaning: serving a master in Igbo apprenticeship

meaning: to learn a trade as an apprentice

Dike anaghị agba ọsọ n'ihu nsogbu.

Isi ike m mere na ndụ enweghị ike ịkwatu m.

Anyaukwu na-emebi ndị mmadụ n'ahịa a.
Ori na-ezu ohi n'abalị mgbe ọ dị ike ịrụ ọrụ.
Ohi enweghị isi, ọrụ ka mma.
Nsogbu abịara, mana m gaghị ada n'ala.
Ahụhụ m tara nyere m aka ịmara uru ego.
Ana m ata ahụhụ ka ụmụ m ghara ịta ya.
Ndụ dị ike, mana onye lara azụ agaghị eri.

meaning: I am exhausted (my strength is finished)
example: Ike gwụrụ m, mana m gaghị akwụsị mgbali.
M na-achọ ego kemgbe chi bọrọ ruo mgbe ọ gbara ọchịchịrị.
Akpa ego m tọgbọrọ chakoo taa.

meaning: a coin, a unit of money
example: Ọ bụ otu mkpụrụ ego fọdụrụ m n'akpa.
Nrọ m niile bụ ije mba ka m gbanwee akụkọ ezinụlọ m.
Njem ego m ga-eduru m ebe m na-eche echiche.
Onye na-arahụ ụra anaghị enweta ọganihu.

Chi m na-eduzi m mgbe ụzọ niile mechiri.

Onye isi na-abụ onye buru ụzọ bilie.

meaning: apprentice boy serving under a master

meaning: eating and drinking; a full feast (lit. food and drink)

meaning: name / reputation (as in making a name at an event)

meaning: come around / come join in (call to gather)

meaning: extremely enjoyable (pidgin intensifier common in Igbo speech)

meaning: this is life / this is living

meaning: forget the troubles / let go of worries

meaning: put your hands up in the air
example: DJ shout make everybody tie aka n'elu.

meaning: let it drop / let it go down (hype call)
example: The beat heavy, ka ọ daa for the dance floor!

meaning: take it easy / dance smoothly (pidgin loan)

meaning: we are the kings / we run this

meaning: honour / respect (shown to a celebrant or elder at an event)

meaning: gather the people / bring everyone together

meaning: God (contracted praise name for the Creator)

Ana m arịọ gị, biko zaa m.
Ka ọ dị otu a, Amen.

meaning: amen / so be it (traditional affirmation of a blessing)

meaning: the Almighty / one of great power

meaning: the things God is doing / God's works
example: Ihe Chukwu na-eme dị ịtụnanya.

Onye nsọ na-ebi ndụ dị ọcha.
Ozi ọma abịawo n'ala anyị.
Kelee Chukwu maka ihe niile.

meaning: Let no one abandon their sibling; look out for your kin
example: Onye aghala nwanne ya, we rise together or not at all.

meaning: Let the kite perch and let the eagle perch — live and let live
example: Egbe bere ugo bere, there is room for me and room for you.

term: Nwata kwocha aka o soro ndi okenye rie nri
meaning: When a child washes their hands well, they dine with elders — merit earns a seat
example: I washed my hands clean, so nwata kwocha aka, now I eat with kings.

meaning: One person alone cannot raise a child — it takes a community
example: Otu onye adighi azu nwa, so bring your hands, we build this thing together.

meaning: May wealth reach home — success should benefit your people
example: Aku ruo ulo, I carry the blessing back to my village.

term: Onye ji onye n'ani ji onwe ya
meaning: He who holds another down holds himself down too
example: Onye ji onye n'ani ji onwe ya, so let go and we both rise.

term: Isi kotara ebu ka ebu na-agba
meaning: The head that disturbs the wasp is the one it stings — you reap what you provoke
example: Isi kotara ebu ka ebu na-agba, don't start what will bite you back.

meaning: A conversation begins from the head — deal with the root first
example: Before we argue, nkata na-esi n'isi, start from the beginning.

meaning: Let a person know themselves — know your limits
example: Onye maara onwe ya, I stay in my lane and shine.

term: Ihe onye metara ka o na-ari
meaning: What one plants is what one climbs — you carry your own actions
example: Ihe onye metara ka o na-ari, so I sow only good seed.

term: Onye hulu ihe nine anaghi eri nri
meaning: One who investigates everything never gets to eat — don't over-scrutinize
example: Relax, onye hulu ihe nine anaghi eri nri, just enjoy the moment.

meaning: The water that swallowed the elephant — a force greater than the mighty
example: Trouble came like mmiri riri enyi and humbled the proud.

term: Onye na-eche ndu ya na-eche akpa ya
meaning: He who guards his life guards his bag too — protect what matters
example: Onye na-eche ndu ya na-eche akpa ya, I watch my back and my bread.

meaning: One's chi (personal god) does not sleep in the daytime — destiny is always watching
example: Chi onye adighi ehie n'ehihie, my star stays awake for me.

meaning: If one agrees, one's chi agrees — belief unlocks destiny
example: Onye kwe chi ya ekwe, so I say yes to my own greatness.

term: Ofu aka ruta manu o zue oha onu
meaning: When one finger touches oil, it soils the others — one bad act spreads
example: Ofu aka ruta manu o zue oha onu, one careless move stains us all.

meaning: Morning is never bad to a person — a fresh start is always welcome
example: Ututu adighi ejo mmadu, today I begin again.

term: Agwo emeghi nke o ji buru agwo
meaning: If the snake shows nothing of what makes it a snake, lizards would ride it — command respect
example: Agwo emeghi nke o ji buru agwo, so I show them who I truly am.

term: Onye ije ka onye isi awo maara
meaning: The traveller knows more than the grey-haired one — experience teaches
example: Onye ije ka onye isi awo maara, I have seen the road myself.

term: Egbe belu ugo belu, nke si ibe ya ebela nku kwaa ya
meaning: Let the kite and eagle perch; whichever denies the other, let its wing break — fairness for all
example: Egbe belu ugo belu, may whoever blocks my brother lose their wing.

term: Onye ije adighi atu egwu ohia
meaning: A traveller does not fear the bush — the determined don't fear the journey
example: Onye ije adighi atu egwu ohia, the road cannot scare me.

term: Nwa nnunu na-amaghi ebe o ga-ebe akwa
meaning: A little bird knowing where it will cry — foreknowledge of trouble
example: Something in me cried like nwa nnunu na-amaghi, I sensed the storm.

meaning: A matter settled with two people — some things are private between few
example: Ejiri mmadu abuo hie okwu, keep this between me and you.

term: Aka nri kwoo aka ekpe, aka ekpe akwoo aka nri
meaning: The right hand washes the left, the left washes the right — mutual help
example: Aka nri kwoo aka ekpe, you lift me and I lift you.

term: Onye ubiam ekwesighi inwe obi mgbawa
meaning: The poor one should not lose heart — hope for the humble
example: Onye ubiam ekwesighi inwe obi mgbawa, my day is surely coming.

meaning: The world is a marketplace — we come, we trade, we leave
example: Uwa bu ahia, so I trade in love while I am here.

meaning: Death does not smell bribe — death takes rich and poor alike
example: Onwu adighi anu nsi, so live good while the sun still shines.

term: Nkita si na nke o ji anya ya abuo
meaning: The dog uses both its eyes — stay fully alert
example: Nkita si na nke o ji anya ya abuo, I keep both eyes on the road.

meaning: One who uses a needle to draw water — a futile, endless labour
example: Chasing that dream was onye were ntutu gbaa mmiri, all sweat no water.

term: Nwata bunie nna ya elu, akpa ya adakwasi ya
meaning: If a child lifts the father up, the father's bag falls on him — some burdens rebound
example: Nwata bunie nna ya elu, take care what you carry above you.

term: Okuko na-aracha nti ka o na-echeta onye gburu nne ya
meaning: The fowl licks its beak and remembers who killed its mother — grievances linger
example: Okuko na-aracha nti, memory does not forget the wound.

term: Ebe onye dara ka chi ya kwaturu ya
meaning: Where a person falls is where their chi pushed them down — accept your setbacks
example: Ebe onye dara, I rise from the very place I fell.

term: Onye buru chi ya uzo, o gbagbue onwe ya n'oso
meaning: He who runs ahead of his chi runs himself to death — don't outrun your destiny
example: Onye buru chi ya uzo, patience, my time will come.

term: Anaghi eji ofu ukwu ama mba
meaning: You don't know a land with one foot — commit fully to understand
example: Anaghi eji ofu ukwu ama mba, I give this my whole heart.

term: Ngweere si na ihe o na-eme ya mgbu dika ihu
meaning: The lizard says what pains it shows on its face — pain reveals itself
example: Ngweere si na ihe o na-eme ya, my face cannot hide this hurt.

meaning: The rat eats and hides some — save for tomorrow
example: Oke okporo na-eri na-ezo, I eat today but keep for hard days.

term: Onye zoro ezo ka a na-agba n' egbe
meaning: The one who hides is the one they shoot at — hiding invites suspicion
example: Come out plain, onye zoro ezo ka a na-agba n'egbe.

meaning: Sweet sleep kills opportunity — laziness costs you
example: Ura na-egbu ochọ, so I wake before the sun to chase mine.

term: Onye nwe ala nwe eluigwe ya
meaning: He who owns the land owns its sky too — root ownership brings full rights
example: Onye nwe ala nwe eluigwe ya, this ground and sky are mine.

meaning: A chick shows its beauty from the tail — potential shows early
example: Nwa okuko mara mma n'odu, I saw greatness in you young.

meaning: A person is the god of their fellow — humans hold power over each other
example: Mmadu bu chi ibe ya, be gentle, your hand can lift or crush.

term: Onye na-akwa aka n'aka anaghi eji aguu daa
meaning: One who keeps working with their hands does not fall from hunger — hustle survives
example: Onye na-akwa aka anaghi eji aguu daa, my hands will feed me.

term: Osisi kwọrọ akwọ na-esi n'ọzọ mepụta
meaning: A dried tree still sprouts anew — renewal after loss
example: Osisi kwọrọ akwọ, watch me grow green again.

term: Onye ọ na-agụ agụụ ka a na-enye nri
meaning: You feed the one who is hungry — help those who truly need it
example: Onye ọ na-agụ agụụ ka a na-enye nri, I give where it counts.

meaning: Where one thing stands, another stands beside it — balance and duality
example: Ihe kwụrụ, ihe akwụdebe ya, joy and struggle walk together.

term: Onye ọ masịrị ka a na-eme
meaning: You do for the one you love — favour goes where affection is
example: Onye ọ masịrị ka a na-eme, for you I go the extra mile.

term: Achọghị m ka a mara m ama, ka a nụ m nụ
meaning: Not just to be seen but to be heard of — reputation that travels
example: Achọghị m ka a mara m ama, let my name ring in far towns.

meaning: The dog follows its owner — loyalty stays with its source
example: Nkịta na-eso onye nwe ya, I ride with the ones who feed me.

meaning: As it is told, so a person learns — wisdom passes through telling
example: Aku na-ako, mmadu na-amuta, sit down and learn the old ways.

meaning: One who shows a person favour — kindness that is remembered
example: Onye na-emenye mmadu ihu n'anya, small kindness plants big roots.

term: Ọ bụghị mgbe niile ka udene na-erughị nsọ
meaning: The vulture is not unclean forever — situations and reputations can change
example: Give it time, ọ bụghị mgbe niile ka udene na-erughị nsọ.

term: Onye ọjọọ na-eche na mmadu niile dị ka ya
meaning: A wicked person thinks everyone is like them — we judge others by ourselves
example: Onye ọjọọ na-eche na mmadu niile dị ka ya, but my heart is clean.

meaning: There is no useless finger — everyone has value
example: Ọ dịghị mkpịsị aka nkịrịsị, every hand in this team matters.

term: Ọ bụ n'ọnụ ka ọkụkụ ji ama mma
meaning: It is the mouth that makes talk sweet — speak well and win people
example: Ọ bụ n'ọnụ ka ọkụkụ ji ama mma, so I choose my words with care.

term: Onye hụrụ ọnyà gwara ọ hụ
meaning: He who sees the trap warns the untrapped — share the warning you have
example: Onye hụrụ ọnyà gwara ọ hụ, I speak so you don't fall too.

term: Ọ bụ otù mkpụrụ osisi ka e ji ama ụtọ ọhịa
meaning: One fruit lets you know the sweetness of the forest — a sample reveals the whole
example: Ọ bụ otù mkpụrụ osisi, taste one and you'll trust the rest.

term: Ewu na-amụ nwa n'oge udummiri, ọ maara ihe ọ ga-eme
meaning: The goat that births in the rainy season knows what it will do — trust that people plan for their situation
example: Ewu na-amụ nwa n'oge udummiri, don't worry, she knows her way.

meaning: No one knows tomorrow — the future is hidden
example: Ọ nweghị onye ma echi, so I live today with open hands.

term: Anụ nyụọ ahụ, ọ maghị onye nyụrụ ya
meaning: When it stinks in a crowd, no one owns the smell — no one claims a shameful act
example: Anụ nyụọ ahụ, everybody looks away when it goes wrong.

term: Onye nwere ndidi na-agọ nwa mmiri
meaning: The patient one milks even a stone — patience wins the impossible
example: Onye nwere ndidi, I wait and the locked door opens.

meaning: hello / greetings (also used for sympathy: sorry)

meaning: good morning (lit. how did you sleep)

term: Komai nisan dare, gari zai waye
meaning: However long the night, day will break — hardship always ends
example: Sit tight, komai nisan dare gari zai waye, our morning is coming.

meaning: Going slowly does not stop you from arriving
example: Take it easy, sannu ba ta hana zuwa, we'll still reach the top.

meaning: Patience is the remedy for the world
example: When the pressure hits, remember hankuri maganin duniya.

term: Kome ka shuka, shi za ka girba
meaning: Whatever you plant, that you will harvest
example: Kome ka shuka shi za ka girba, so I'm sowing only good.

term: Rijiya ba ta cika da raɓa
meaning: A well is not filled by dew — small effort won't finish a big job
example: One coin won't build it, rijiya ba ta cika da raɓa.

term: Da sannu-sannu akan kai wuri mai nisa
meaning: Slowly, slowly one reaches a far place
example: Da sannu-sannu akan kai wuri mai nisa, step by step, I climb.

meaning: Kinship is kept alive by the feet — you must visit to keep ties
example: Come see me, zumunta a ƙafa take, don't be a stranger.

term: Ba a hana jaki shan ruwa saboda kunya
meaning: A donkey isn't stopped from drinking out of shyness — don't let shame block your needs
example: Ask for what's yours, ba a hana jaki shan ruwa saboda kunya.

term: Kaza ta fi zakara wayo wajen kwan ƙwai
meaning: The hen is wiser than the cock at laying eggs — each has their own expertise
example: Let her lead this, kaza ta fi zakara wayo wajen kwan ƙwai.

meaning: Greed is the key to suffering
example: Don't grab it all, kwaɗayi mabuɗin wahala, my brother.

meaning: Truth is wider than a calabash — truth outweighs everything
example: Say it plain, gaskiya ta fi ƙwarya faɗi in the end.

meaning: Truth is a horse of iron — it never tires or breaks
example: Ride the truth, gaskiya dokin ƙarfe, it carries you home.

meaning: Shame is worse than death — dignity matters greatly
example: Guard your name, kunya ta fi mutuwa, they say.

meaning: Useless work does not fill the well
example: Put in real effort, aikin banza baya cika rijiya.
Blood stays blood, zumunci ba ya ƙarewa forever.

term: In ba ka san inda za ka ba, ka san inda ka fito
meaning: If you don't know where you're going, know where you came from
example: Remember your roots, in ba ka san inda za ka ba, ka san inda ka fito.

term: Wanda ya yi haƙuri ya ci nasara
meaning: The one who is patient wins victory
example: Hold on a little, wanda ya yi haƙuri ya ci nasara.

meaning: One bird does not make a flock — unity is strength
example: We move together, tsuntsu daya baya yin garka.

meaning: One hand cannot lift the roof — we need each other
example: Help me carry this, hannu daya baya ɗaukar jinka.

term: Ruwan da ya zube ba a tara shi
meaning: Spilled water cannot be gathered again — some things can't be undone
example: What's done is done, ruwan da ya zube ba a tara shi.

term: Bakin mutum shi ke sa masa wuya
meaning: A person's own mouth brings them trouble
example: Watch your tongue, bakin mutum shi ke sa masa wuya.
I gave my word, alkawari bashi ne, I will pay it.

term: Yaro baya sanin yaƙi sai ya tashi
meaning: A child doesn't know war until he grows — experience teaches
example: You'll understand later, yaro baya sanin yaƙi sai ya tashi.

meaning: Stinginess does not prevent death — hoarding saves no one
example: Share while you live, rowa ba ta hana mutuwa.

term: Duk mai wutsiya ba ya wasa da wuta
meaning: Whoever has a tail does not play with fire — know your weakness
example: Stay careful, duk mai wutsiya ba ya wasa da wuta.

term: Idan rijiya ta ƙafe, sai a haƙa wata
meaning: If the well dries up, dig another — find a new way
example: Plan B is ready, idan rijiya ta ƙafe sai a haƙa wata.

term: Mai gida daya, shi ke jin wari
meaning: The one in the house alone smells its stench — insiders know the truth
example: Only we know the struggle, mai gida daya shi ke jin wari.

meaning: A rabbit doesn't smell its own fur — we can't see our own faults
example: Check yourself first, zomo baya jin ƙamshin gashinsa.
Only forward for us, cin gaba ba tsayawa, no reverse.

meaning: Bathed in victory — steeped in success
example: We came out wanka da nasara this year.

term: Kowa ya ga daɗi, ya ga wahala
meaning: Whoever tastes sweetness has also seen hardship
example: No pain no gain, kowa ya ga daɗi ya ga wahala.
Earn it clean, sata ba ta wadata, it never lasts.

term: Da farin wata a kan gan shi
meaning: By the moon's brightness it is seen — the truth eventually shows
example: Time will reveal it, da farin wata a kan gan shi.

meaning: A lie only blossoms, it bears no fruit — falsehood leads nowhere
example: Truth wins in the end, karya fure take ba ta 'ya'ya.

meaning: Little by little the bird builds its nest
example: Brick by brick, kaɗan-kaɗan tsuntsu ke gina shekarsa.

term: In ka ci gindin mai gida, ka rasa masauki
meaning: If you betray your host, you lose your lodging — don't bite the hand that feeds
example: Respect the house, in ka ci gindin mai gida ka rasa masauki.

term: Damina ba ta zuwa ba tare da gizagizai ba
meaning: The rains don't come without clouds — every result has its signs
example: Watch the signs, damina ba ta zuwa ba tare da gizagizai ba.

meaning: Every bird has its own song — everyone has their own gift
example: Sing your truth, kowane tsuntsu da waƙarsa.

term: Rana ba ta ɓoyewa da hannu daya
meaning: The sun cannot be hidden with one hand — truth can't be covered
example: They can't hide it, rana ba ta ɓoyewa da hannu daya.

meaning: The ear is older than the grandfather — heed news and warnings
example: I heard it all, kunne ya girmi kaka, nothing surprises me.

meaning: Not night, not day — working nonstop, around the clock
example: We grind ba dare ba rana for this dream.

meaning: The world is a marketplace — everyone comes and goes
example: Do your trade well, duniya kasuwa ce, we all leave.

term: Mai haƙuri kan dafa dutse ya sha romonsa
meaning: The patient one cooks a stone and drinks its broth — patience achieves the impossible
example: Just wait, mai haƙuri kan dafa dutse ya sha romonsa.
skip

term: Kura ba ta cin ɗan uwanta
meaning: A hyena does not eat its own kind — family protects family
example: We look after our own, kura ba ta cin ɗan uwanta.

term: Idan ka ga gemun ɗan uwanka ya kama wuta, ka ba naka ruwa
meaning: If your neighbor's beard catches fire, wet your own — heed others' warnings
example: Learn from their fall, idan ka ga gemun ɗan uwanka ya kama wuta ka ba naka ruwa.

term: Nono da madara duk fari ne
meaning: Milk and cream are both white — looks can hide difference
example: Look deeper, nono da madara duk fari ne, not all is the same.

term: Uwar shanu ba ta jin nauyin ƙahonta
meaning: The mother cow doesn't feel the weight of her horns — love bears its own burden
example: I carry you gladly, uwar shanu ba ta jin nauyin ƙahonta.

meaning: A person's character is their true wealth
example: Stay good, halin mutum shi ne ummarsa, character is everything.

term: Ba a kokawa da mai ƙaƙarƙashin ƙasa
meaning: You don't wrestle the one who knows the ground — respect the master's turf
example: Not on his home ground, ba a kokawa da mai ƙaƙarƙashin ƙasa.

term: Da hannunka ka jefa kanka a rijiya
meaning: With your own hand you threw yourself in the well — self-inflicted trouble
example: You chose this road, da hannunka ka jefa kanka a rijiya.

meaning: Water doesn't turn sour for nothing — there's always a reason
example: Something's wrong here, ruwa ba su tsami banza.

term: Kome tsawon rai, mutuwa ce ƙarshe
meaning: However long life is, death is the end
example: Live upright, kome tsawon rai mutuwa ce ƙarshe.

meaning: God has more power than anyone
example: I trust the plan, Allah ya fi kowa iko over all.

term: Neman sani ba shi da iyaka
meaning: The search for knowledge has no limit
example: Keep learning, neman sani ba shi da iyaka, till the end.

term: Zaman lafiya ya fi zama dan sarki
meaning: Living in peace is better than living like a prince
example: Give me calm, zaman lafiya ya fi zama dan sarki any day.

term: Abin da ke cikin randa shi ke ɓuɓɓuga
meaning: What is inside the pot is what bubbles out — the heart reveals itself
example: Your words show your heart, abin da ke cikin randa shi ke ɓuɓɓuga.

term: Bakon abu ba ya wuce kwana uku
meaning: A strange thing does not last beyond three days — novelty fades
example: Give it time, bakon abu ba ya wuce kwana uku.
skip

term: Kwana ba ya gushewa sai da safiya
meaning: A night does not pass except by morning — endurance brings dawn
example: Hold on friend, kwana ba ya gushewa sai da safiya.

term: Doki mai kyau, ba ya buƙatar ƙarin bulala
meaning: A good horse doesn't need extra whipping — the willing need no pushing
example: She's ready already, doki mai kyau ba ya buƙatar ƙarin bulala.

term: Idan an yi maka rijiya, ka yi wa wani
meaning: If someone digs a well for you, dig one for another — pay kindness forward
example: Pass the blessing on, idan an yi maka rijiya ka yi wa wani.

term: Wanda bai ji ba, ya gani
meaning: Whoever won't listen will see — hard heads learn the hard way
example: I warned you first, wanda bai ji ba ya gani.

meaning: friend, buddy; also a filler exclamation like 'man!'
example: Chale, this beat go make everybody dance tonight.

meaning: how are you? / how is it going? (Twi greeting)
example: Ɛte sɛn, my people, I dey miss you all.

meaning: it is good / it's fine / all is well
example: Everything eye, we go win this one.

meaning: love / my love / darling (term of endearment)
example: Ɔdɔ, come make I hold you close tonight.
Medaase for staying by my side through it all.
Akwaaba to my city, come feel the vibe.

meaning: my guy, let's go / are you coming? (casual call to move)
example: Charle wote, the party dey start already.

meaning: exclamation of surprise, shock or excitement — 'wow!' / 'hey!'
example: Herh! See how she move on that dance floor.
You get the deal? Saa? Then we dey chop life.

meaning: exclamation of mild frustration or 'come on!'
example: Aba, stop wasting my time and dance.

meaning: they call me / my name is (used when introducing yourself)
example: Yɛ frɛ me the king of this sound.
The obroni them came all the way to feel our rhythm.
Leave the kokonsa, focus on your own shine.

meaning: very / a lot (intensifier, placed after a word)
example: I love you paa, no be small thing.
Massa, this life sweet when we together.
No wahala tonight, just make we enjoy.
Dabi, I no fit leave you behind.
Aane, tell me say you feel the same.
Small boy grow to sikanii, watch me now.
Hard work go bring the sika, just wait.
Wherever you dey, my efie be with you.

meaning: no way / not at all (emphatic no)
example: Give up? Aba dabi, we push till the end.

meaning: street guys / the boys / regular hustlers
example: The boys boys them dey outside vibing to my track.
Don't turn my night into yawa, behave yourself.
Ampa, this love no dey fade away.

meaning: we have God / God is with us
example: Whatever come, yebi wo Nyame, we go stand.
Nyame bless the road that bring me to you.
No be kwasea I be, I know your game.
Send the love like momo, straight to my heart.
From the trotro to the stage, na my story.

meaning: cassava flour food staple; slang for basic sustenance
example: Even with just gari we still dey smile.
Sunday morning, waakye and good music.
Aden you dey run from my love?
Mepa wo kyɛw, give me one more dance.
When I dey far, kae me every night.
Bra, make we lose ourselves in this sound.
No kɔ yet, the night still young.

meaning: give it to me / hand it over
example: Fa ma me your heart, I go keep am safe.
The city dey call, yɛnkɔ tonight.
Obiaa dey feel this rhythm in their bones.
Adɛn nti you dey doubt what we build?
You love me, enti make we shine together.
Kafra if my love come too strong for you.
They doubt me? Twɛa, watch me rise.

meaning: human being / mortal (reminds of shared humanity)
example: Me too I be odesani, I feel the pain.
Boa me carry this dream to the top.
The show dey Friday, wobɛba make we jam?
Onua, we rise from the same soil.
Ei! This vibe too sweet, I no fit sit down.

meaning: how is your body / how are you feeling?
example: Long time, wo ho te sɛn, my old friend?
Home cooking, hot nkwan and family round the pot.

meaning: young man / gentleman (often a compliment on a sharp guy)
example: The abrantie step out fresh, everybody dey look.
That ɔbaa move like the music dey inside her.
Make some dede, the whole street dey vibe.
I dey give you nokware, nothing but real.

meaning: stop it / leave it / quit that
example: Gyae the doubt and dance with me.
Bɛn me small, let the rhythm hold us.
Yoo, we go do am your way tonight.
Wo ɔdɔ na ɛma me nyane anɔpa biara.
Medɔ wo, na me nso mennim ase a mede kyerɛ no.
Me dɔfo, bra na twɛn me wɔ ɔkwan no so.
Ɔdɔfoɔ a wote m'akoma mu no, wo na mefrɛ.
M'akoma bɔ den bere biara a mihu wo.
Wokɔɔ no, na m'akoma abu mmienu.

meaning: I long for you, I crave you
example: Anadwo dɔɔso no me kɔn dɔ wo ara kwa.
Me kɔn dɔ da a yɛbɛhyia bio.
Me kra, wo ne me nkwa mu hann.
Adɔeɛ a wokyerɛ me no boro biribiara so.

meaning: love is death — proverb for love's overwhelming power
example: Wɔka sɛ ɔdɔ yɛ owuo, na afei mahu ase.
Wo kɔ no gyaw awerɛhoɔ wɔ me tirim.
Da bi mɛware wo wɔ nnipa nyinaa anim.
Yɛn awareɛ da no bɛyɛ anigyeɛ kɛseɛ.
Me yere, wo na woma me fie yɛ hann.
Me kunu, mepɛ sɛ yɛnyin nkyɛ.

meaning: lover, paramour (often an illicit or secret partner)
example: Wɔse ɔwɔ mpena wɔ kurow foforɔ no mu.

meaning: to date, to court, to have a lover
example: Yɛn mmienu retwe mpena afe abien ni.

meaning: I like you, I want you
example: Me pɛ wo firi da a mihuu wo kane.
Wo ho yɛ fɛ te sɛ ɔsram a apue.
Wo ahoɔfɛ no ma m'ano to mu.
Me dɔ wo paa, na ɛnyɛ nsɛm hunu.

meaning: I love you from the bottom of my heart
example: Medɔ wo firi m'akoma mu, ɛnyɛ nkontompo.
Wo ba ma anigyeɛ hyɛ me fie ma.

meaning: I delight in you, you make me glad
example: Bere biara m'ani gye wo ho sɛ ahemfie.
M'ɔhemmaa, tena m'akyi na yɛnkɔ.

meaning: my gold, my treasure (term of endearment)
example: Me sika, wo som bo kyɛn ade nyinaa.
Dɔ me sɛnea mɛdɔ wo no ara.

meaning: come to my side, come close to me
example: Bra me nkyɛn na ma yɛnka nsɛm kakra.
Gye me sɛ wo dea daa daa.
Sɛ wokɔ akyirikyiri a, kae me.
Nokware dɔ nsesa bere biara mu.
Mennye ɔdɔ atorɔ nto mu bio.
Yɛn ntam ɔdɔ kɛseɛ na ɛda hɔ.

meaning: love, darling (common informal spelling of ɔdɔ)
example: Odo, frɛ me bere a woduru fie.

meaning: lady, madam (respectful, affectionate address for a woman)
example: Awuraa, ma me kwan na menfa wo nsa.

meaning: my heart is at peace, I am relieved and calm
example: Sɛ wo ho ba a, me bo tɔ me yam.
Wo ne ɔdɔfopa a mahwehwɛ no.
Sɛ owuo ba mpo a, me werɛ mfiri wo da.
Yɛnse yɛnnɔ, na mmom yɛnyɛ no adeyɛ mu.
Nnɛ, ɔkyena, medɔ wo daa.
Obaa pa te sɛ wo yɛ na ɛho hia.
Barima pa hwɛ ne yere so yie.
Me dɔ, fa kyɛ me na mensan nyɛ bio.
Ɛmfa ho asɛm no, yɛnnɔ nkɔ so.
Me pa wo kyɛw, nnyaw me hɔ.
Anadwo no nyinaa m'ani so nnaa wo nti.
Ga mli lɛ, suɔmɔ ji nɔ ni fĩɔ shĩa.
Misumɔ bo, ni misumɔ bo be fɛɛ be.
Osɛ hã mi miishɛɛ babaoo daa gbi.

meaning: I'm attracted to you, I have feelings for you (Ghanaian Pidgin)
example: Since the party, I dey feel you well well.
Charley, you be the only one for my heart.
My dear, make we waka this life together.

meaning: to enjoy romance, to bask in being loved (Ghanaian Pidgin)
example: Come make we chop love before morning reach.

meaning: to start developing romantic feelings (Ghanaian Pidgin)
example: I no plan am but I catch feelings for you.
No lie, you be my everything for this world.

meaning: close friend/companion — used tenderly for a partner (Ghanaian Pidgin)
example: You be my paddy and my lover all in one.
Me dɔfoɔ pa, wo ho hia me sen sika.
Fa wo ho ma me na mede me ho ma wo.
No sika, no move — I dey chase am today.
Chalé, this hustle no dey sleep.
I dey grind paa, dawn till dark.
He dey do kalabule with the fuel and the whole street vex.

meaning: a Ghanaian who made money abroad and came back flexing
example: Every borga land with fresh kicks and loud stories.
I no fit be kobolo — I get mouths to feed.

meaning: shared minibus, the broke man's transport
example: Pocket dry so na trotro carry me back to the ends.
The obroni contract hit and my whole crew smiled.

meaning: animal — insult for a fool or brute
example: That aboa tried to dupe me but I saw am coming.
Leave the kokonsa, keep your eye on the bag.

meaning: Ga rally cry — leader shouts it, crowd answers 'yei!'
example: When the deal closed I shouted tsooboi and the boys roared back.

meaning: a flashy, scantily-dressed slay girl about town
example: The apuskeleke them line the strip when the money boys arrive.

meaning: rough motor-park tout who collects fares by force
example: The agbero for the station no dey let cedi pass am.

meaning: a hired heavy, muscle-for-hire tough guy
example: They roll with a macho so nobody test them for the market.
Checked my pocket, na gbee stare back at me.

meaning: money sent from abroad, diaspora cash
example: The borga sika drop and Mama roof finally get fixed.

meaning: to eat; also to spend, enjoy, or embezzle money
example: Work hard first, then we go chop the profit clean.
Before I flex I make sure the chop money reach house.

meaning: the on-off power blackouts that choke the hustle
example: Dumsor kill my shop today but I still counted small profit.

meaning: goat — dig at a greedy or dull person
example: No be aponkye way go swallow the whole share alone.
Small small I drop for susu till the target land.
Throw the bola excuses, show me the money.

meaning: to hustle, scramble, or scrape money together fast
example: I saka up and down before the rent day catch me.

meaning: a female head-porter who carries loads in the market for coins
example: The kayayo carry the world on her head for few cedis.

meaning: cheap rice-and-beans street food, breakfast of the grind
example: Cold morning, hot waakye, then straight to the hustle.

meaning: take heart, calm down, be patient
example: Fa wo bo to ho, the breakthrough dey come.

meaning: the folks, my people, the crew
example: Yaanom, we rise together or we no rise at all.

meaning: how are you doing / how's the hustle
example: Chalé wo ho te sɛn, money dey enter?

meaning: I want money / I'm after money
example: No time for stories, mepɛ sika this year.
The night strip get its own hard hustle after dark.

meaning: backside, buttocks (vulgar); also 'the back end' of things
example: Watch your trumu, this street no dey forgive slackers.
Only a kwasea go trust a plug he never test.
Cut the gyimii, we get bills to clear.
The market turn basaa when police raid the boys.

meaning: a pesewa — the smallest coin, symbol of being dead broke
example: I count am to the last pɛsɛwa before I sleep.

meaning: scam, fraud, a con job (azaa = fake/deceit)
example: That azaa offer sweet too much — na trap be that.

meaning: internet fraud / online scamming (often with juju undertone)
example: Some boys chase sakawa money, me I chase clean cedis.
The grind no get weekend for this side of town.

meaning: one's hood, the neighbourhood you rep
example: I blow big but I still show face for the ends.

meaning: the rough poor quarters you're trying to climb out of
example: From the trenches to the top, na so we dey move.

meaning: a smart, streetwise sharp fellow (as in 'be guy')
example: You go be guy for this town or the town go chop you.
Broke on my birthday be big yawa, chalé.
My aviɛ hold me down when the hustle turn cold.
Show up with the shashi and the doors go open.

meaning: the Ghana cedi, the currency itself
example: Every cedi I get, half go straight to the plan.

meaning: to push, shove, or push forward (also to push a hustle)
example: We pia the goods till the last one sell.
Sharp boot jacket, small price, big flex.

meaning: secondhand clothes (lit. 'the white man has died')
example: I dey sell obroni wawu but I dress like a boss.
Them settle the tumtum deals when nobody dey watch.

meaning: cheap rubber slippers, the everyman's footwear
example: Charley wote for now, designer shoes when the bag land.

meaning: things are hard here / life is tough
example: Aha yɛ den but I no go bow.

meaning: it's hot / it's tense (situation heating up)
example: When police enter, the market ɛyɛ hye sharp.

meaning: exclamation of shock, surprise or dismay
example: Eii, they raise the rent again this year?

meaning: boss, big man, sir (respect or sarcasm)
example: Small hustle today, big masa tomorrow.

meaning: street cash, quick money from the corner
example: Grab small kae now, plan the big bag later.

meaning: call-and-response hype chant to rally a crowd
example: Money in hand, I lead the chooboi and they answer loud.

meaning: since way back, from the very beginning
example: Tɛtɛɛtɛ I dey hustle this same corner.

meaning: party, celebration when the money lands
example: Deal close, so tonight na pati for the whole ends.

meaning: I've got it / it's within my grasp now
example: After long grind, me nsa aka the dream.

meaning: watch yourself / take care of yourself out here
example: Streets rough, chalé, hwɛ wo ho so.

meaning: high-energy group call-and-response singing/chanting that hypes a crowd
example: Once the jama started, the whole hall was jumping.

meaning: Ga exclamation of hype meaning 'boom'/'bang', used to punch up energy
example: Drop the beat and everybody shout gbee!

meaning: popular Ghanaian dance style with expressive hand gestures
example: She hit that azonto and the floor went crazy.
We were skanking till the DJ killed the lights.

meaning: literally 'it is cooked here' — the party is peaking, the vibe is ready
example: Come through tonight, aben wɔ ha for real.

meaning: 'it's you!' — praise shouted to hype someone up
example: When she walked in fresh, everybody yelled woara.

meaning: 'buddy/mate' — friendly address, common opener among friends
example: Charle, this party is mad, we no dey leave.

meaning: Ga for 'friend, let's go' — a rallying phrase to move out and turn up
example: Chale wote, the club opens by ten.

meaning: to party hard and bring maximum energy
example: We came to turn up, no be to sit down.

meaning: Ga term for a loud, packed, rowdy gathering or wild crowd
example: By midnight the whole place was one big gbese.

meaning: Ga slang for enjoyment, good times, having fun
example: Weekend na for jɔmɔ, we dey outside.

meaning: to push/shove your way — used for pushing into the dancefloor vibe
example: Pia small make we reach the front.

meaning: a returnee from abroad who flaunts money and style at parties
example: The boga rolled in and started spraying cash.

meaning: to shower money on a performer or celebrant
example: They spray her all night till the floor was covered.

meaning: hustle/wheeling-dealing energy; loosely used for chaotic lively scene
example: The night had that sweet kalabule buzz.
This jam sweet paa, I no fit sit down.

meaning: Twi-pidgin 'you want it?' teasing hype phrase on the floor
example: DJ ask the crowd, wompe? and everybody scream.

meaning: onomatopoeic 'quick-quick / hustle' vibe, fast lively movement
example: Kpakpakpa, we dey move from club to club tonight.

meaning: stay alert and enjoy sharply; be switched on
example: Shine your eye, the real party start after midnight.

meaning: pidgin noun for pure fun and pleasure of the moment
example: Tonight na pure enjoyment, forget your worries.

meaning: energy, drive, gusto — dance with vim
example: Put vim inside am, the beat dey call you.

meaning: a sly, tricky dance move; playful showoff step
example: He pulled a kwaku ananse move and the girls laughed.

meaning: call-and-response cheer of triumph and hype
example: The MC shouted osɛe yei and the crowd roared back.

meaning: crowd affirmation shout to build momentum
example: Everybody put your hands up and shout yeah!
Auntie stood up and did kotasa like the old days.

meaning: a viral suggestive Ghanaian dance done in a tight spot
example: They started one corner and cleared the whole room.

meaning: a sweet twisty candy; used to describe a sweet, curvy vibe/girl
example: That dress sweet like alewa, she own the night.

meaning: a wild all-out night out; vulgar undertone (nightlife/red-light)
